note:
新基金已经开始对早期公司进行投资，并计划在未来四年内对多达45家公司进行投资。投资范围从25万欧元到250万欧元。紧随Frontline最近在美国设立的新的成长阶段基金Frontline X，该基金面向希望扩展到欧洲和欧盟地区的美国初创公司，因为爱尔兰是欧盟成员国，并且可以从英国退欧的影响中受益。
 合伙人威廉·麦奎伦（William McQuillan）表示，该基金将把约50％的基金投资到新成立的早期公司。剩余的资金将延迟用于以后对现有投资组合公司的投资。
  McQuillan认为，欧洲拥有全球B2B软件市场的26％，而美国则占50％至55％，因此欧洲拥有大量的增长机会。
 新的种子基金是在美国风险投资公司扎根欧洲的时候发起的，例如去年在伦敦开设办事处的红杉资本（Sequoia），以及最近的通用催化剂（General Catalyst）。 2020年5月，Frontline与Kleiner Perkins，Index和Sequoia一起投资了爱尔兰总部的Evervault公司。
  自2012年以来，Frontline的种子投资公司中有70％从美国的风险投资公司筹集了资金，并且Frontline的合作伙伴在Google，Twitter，SurveyMonkey，Airtable和Yammer等公司都有经验。
 McQuillan在一份声明中说：“当我们回顾2012年的数据时-Frontline刚开始时-很痛苦地表明，欧洲企业家缺乏在欧洲以外建立全球业务的基础设施和支持。如今，欧洲理所当然地跻身美国顶级投资者的目标名单，但全球扩张仍然是需要解决的重要挑战。作为一个团队，我们汇集了所有经验和资源来帮助我们的创始人跨越大西洋。种子基金III将是我们工作的延伸–帮助创始人起步–走向全球。”
 Frontline最引人注目的投资包括Linked Finance，Clearbanc和Currencyfair。 Frontline X成长基金已经投资了TripActions系列B； People.ai的1亿美元C轮融资；以及Clearbanc的5,000万美元B轮融资。 
值得注意的出口包括Logentries，该公司被Rapid7收购； Orchestrate，被CenturyLink收购； 和Pointy在2020年被Google收购。
note_en:
The new fund has already begun to invest in early-stage companies, and is aiming at investing in up to 45 companies over the next four years. Investments will range from €250,000 to €2.5 million. It follows Frontline’s recent new U.S.-based growth-stage fund, Frontline X, which is geared to U.S. startups wanting to expand to Europe and the EU region, given that Ireland is an EU member, and well placed to benefit from the ramifications of Brexit.
 William McQuillan, partner, said the fund would invest about 50% of the fund into new early-stage companies. The remaining funds will be delayed for later investments in existing portfolio companies.
  It’s McQuillan’s view that, with Europe having 26% of all the global B2B software market and the U.S. at 50 to 55%, Europe has plenty of growth opportunities.
 The new seed fund launches at a time when U.S. VC firms are putting down roots in Europe, such as Sequoia, which opened an office in London last year, and more recently General Catalyst. In May of 2020, Frontline invested in Irish HQ’d company Evervault, alongside Kleiner Perkins, Index and Sequoia.
  Seventy percent of Frontline’s seed portfolio companies have raised capital from U.S. VCs since 2012, and Frontline partners have experience with companies such as Google, Twitter, SurveyMonkey, Airtable and Yammer.
 In a statement, McQuillan said, “When we looked at the data back in 2012 – at the very start of Frontline – it was painfully clear that European entrepreneurs lacked the infrastructure and support to build a global business out of Europe. Today, Europe rightfully finds itself on top-tier US investors’ target list, but global expansion remains an important challenge to solve. As a team, we’ve pooled all of our experience and resources into helping our founders cross the Atlantic. Seed Fund III will be an extension of our work – to help founders get off the ground – and go global.”
 Frontline’s more notable investments include Linked Finance, Clearbanc and Currencyfair. The Frontline X growth fund has invested in the Series B of TripActions; People.ai’s $100 million Series C; and Clearbanc’s $50 million Series B.
 Notable exits include Logentries, which was acquired by Rapid7; Orchestrate, acquired by CenturyLink; and Pointy acquired by Google in 2020.

note:
除投资外，该平台还允许客户通过与具有影响力的比较的合作伙伴关系来花费现金，并通过订阅来抵消其碳足迹。核心业务模型为每位客户每月1英镑，另加3,000英镑以上资产的0.30％。例如，碳补偿等附加产品将根据所选等级作为单独的附加订阅收费。
 这家初创公司表示，它在英国的用户数量接近100,000，并且吸引了千禧一代的受众，其中90％的用户“从未投资过”（他们说），这些用户平均每月投资250英镑。
   该应用程序不具有快速“进出”功能，不算作交易应用程序，而是在增加财富的同时投资于具有高影响力的公司的多元化投资组合。它的竞争对手包括MoneyBox，但Tickr表示，相比之下，它是“ 100％纯影响力重点”。绝大多数欧洲人不投资市场，因此这可能是该产品的好机会。
 创始人汤姆·麦吉利库迪（Tom McGillycuddy）和马特·拉瑟姆（Matt Latham）在投资管理领域工作了8年，但表示他们对行话，高额费用和对环境等原因的冷漠感到失望。
 在文字采访中，McGillycuddy告诉我：“我们还意识到人们对投资的潜在影响几乎没有考虑；这纯粹是关于回报。来自威根（Wigan）和利物浦（Liverpool）的我们是家庭中第一个接触这个世界的人，这似乎不对。”两人开始进行影响力投资，随后于2018年推出了Tickr。
note_en:
As well as investing, the platform allows customers to spend their cash via partnerships with impact-oriented compares, and offset their carbon footprint through a subscription. The core business model is £1 p/m per customer, plus 0.30% on assets above £3,000. Additional products, like carbon offsets, for example, are charged as a separate additional subscription depending on the tier selected.
 The startup says it is approaching 100,000 users in the U.K. and is reaching a millennial audience, 90% of which have “never invested before” (they say) and these users are investing £250 per month on average.
   The app is not billed as a trading app, with quick “in and outs”, but is about building wealth whilst investing in a diversified portfolio of high-impact companies. Its competitors include  MoneyBox, but Tickr says it is “100% pure impact focus” by contrast. The vast majority of Europeans don’t invest in markets, so this could be a good opportunity for the product.
 Founders Tom McGillycuddy and Matt Latham spent eight years working in investment management, but say they became disillusioned by the jargon, high fees and indifference to causes such as the environment.
 Over text interview, McGillycuddy told me: “We also realized there was zero consideration for the underlying impact of the investments people were making; it was purely about the return. Coming from Wigan and Liverpool, we were the first people in our families to be exposed to this world, and it didn’t seem right.” The pair moved into impact investing and subsequently went on to launch Tickr in 2018.
title: Tickr，允许散户投资者支持具有影响力的公司，从Ada Ventures获得了340万美元的投资
title_en: Tickr, which allows retail investors to back impact companies, secures $3.4M from Ada Ventures

note:
但是，该公司如何设法吸引投资者以这种敏捷性来进行资金投资一直是一种好奇。 Robinhood如此引人注目，以至于给它注入数十亿美元是一个显而易见的决定吗？
  交易所探索创业公司，市场和资金。每天早上在Extra Crunch上阅读该书，或每个星期六获取The Exchange通讯。
  解析Robinhood的2020年第4季度订单流量付款（PFOF）数据时，我们大为吃惊，这表明折扣交易服务的第3季度业绩增长良好。 Robinhood的PFOF收入增长在2020年第三季度连续下降，但最后一个季度引起了近期的担忧，即独角兽的增长日已经落后。
 但是后来公司给了我们更多，一些图表，我认为更好地解释了为什么罗宾汉能够这么快地筹集到这么多钱。
  Robinhood之所以能够迅速筹集更多现金，是因为该公司的PFOF收入驱动因素可能在GameStop时期的混乱时期变得超载，这在很多人进行交易的时候很明显。
 但是，由于该公司在自己的博客上发布的新图表，我们现在知道Robinhood的PFOF收入可能会飙升至历史新高。
 这是该公司发布的图表，我已按季度间隔粗略地标记了该图表。根据罗宾汉（Robinhood）的说法，绿线是“到上周的更长时期内的罗宾汉股票和期权交易量：”
note_en:
But how the company managed to get investors to wire money with such alacrity has been a curiosity; what about Robinhood was  so compelling that giving it a multibillion dollar injection was such an obvious decision?
  The Exchange explores startups, markets and money. Read it  every morning on Extra Crunch, or get  The Exchange newsletter every Saturday.
  We got a whiff of it when we parsed Robinhood’s Q4 2020 payment for order flow (PFOF) data, which showed the discount trading service growing nicely from its Q3 results. Robinhood’s PFOF revenue growth had  slowed in sequential terms in the third quarter of 2020, but the final quarter iced near-term concerns that the unicorn’s growth days were behind it.
 But then the company gave us a little more, a few charts that I think better explain why Robinhood was able to raise so much money so quickly.
  The reason why Robinhood was able to raise lots more cash very quickly was because the company’s PFOF revenue driver likely went into overdrive during the mess that was the GameStop period, This is somewhat obvious, as many people were trading.
 But thanks to a new chart from the company  posted on its own blog, we now know that Robinhood’s PFOF incomes were likely spiking to all-time highs.
 Here’s the chart the company published, which I have loosely marked with quarterly intervals. Per Robinhood, the green line is “Robinhood equities and options trading volumes over a longer time horizon, through last week:”

 加入我们参加TechCrunch会议：正义，进行广泛的讨论，我们尝试回答这些问题，并与Cynthia Bennett，Mara Mills和Srin Madipalli进一步探讨包容性设计。
  辛西娅·贝内特（Cynthia Bennett）是卡内基梅隆大学人机交互研究所的博士后，也是苹果公司的研究员。她的研究重点是人机交互，可访问性和残障研究，并且在她的网站上说，它涵盖了“ HCI理论和方法的批评和发展，以设计与技术的新兴可访问性交互。”她的研究包括传记原型：重新构想设计中的识别和残障，以及移情的承诺：设计，残障和了解“其他”。
 玛拉·米尔斯（Mara Mills）是纽约大学媒体，文化和传播学副教授，也是纽约大学残疾研究中心的联合创始人和联合主任。 Mills的研究重点是声音研究，残疾研究和历史。 （您可以听到她与AI Now学院和纽约大学Minderoo研究教授的联合创始人梅雷迪思·惠特克（Meredith Whittaker）以及奥林工程学院教授，​​最近出版的《什么可以身体做：我们如何遇见建筑世界，在此处的TechCrunch Mixtape播客上。）
  Srin Madipalli是Accomable的投资者和联合创始人，Accomable是一个在线平台，可帮助用户找到无障碍度假物业，并将其出售给Airbnb。他的倡导工作侧重于包容残疾。请确保在这里以5美元的价格抢购门票。
 请确保您可以加入我们进行此次对话，并参加3月3日举行的TC会议：正义大会。
note_en:
At TechCrunch Sessions: Justice on March 3, we will examine the importance of ensuring accessible product design from the beginning. We’ll ask how the social and medical models of disability influence technological evolution. Integrating the expertise of disabled technologists, makers, investors, scientists and software engineers into the DNA of your company from the very beginning is vital to the pursuit of a functioning and equitable society. And could mean you don’t leave money on the table.
 Join us at  TechCrunch Sessions: Justice for a wide-ranging discussion as we attempt to answer these questions and further explore inclusive design with Cynthia Bennett, Mara Mills and Srin Madipalli.
  Cynthia Bennett is a post-doc at  Carnegie Mellon University’s Human-Computer Interaction Institute, as well as a researcher at Apple. Her research focuses on human-computer interaction, accessibility and  Disability Studies, and, she says on her website, spans “the critique and development of HCI theory and methods to designing emergent accessible interactions with technology.” Her research includes  Biographical Prototypes: Reimagining Recognition and Disability in Design and  The Promise of Empathy: Design, Disability, and Knowing the “Other.”
 Mara Mills is the Associate Professor of Media, Culture, and Communication at New York University and a co-founder and co-director of the  NYU Center for Disability Studies. Mills’ research focuses on sound studies, disability studies and history. (You can hear her discuss the intersection of artificial intelligence and disability with Meredith Whittaker, co-founder of the  AI Now Institute and Minderoo Research Professor at NYU, and Sara Hendren, professor at Olin College of Engineering and author of the recently published  What Can a Body Do: How We Meet the Built World, on the TechCrunch Mixtape podcast  here.)
  Srin Madipalli is an investor and co-founder of Accomable, an online platform that helped users find accessible vacation properties, which he sold to Airbnb. His advocacy work focuses on disability inclusion. Be sure to snag your tickets for  just $5 here.
 Make sure you can join us for this conversation and more at  TC Sessions: Justice on March 3.  Secure your seat now!
title: 在TechCrunch会议上了解无障碍产品设计的重要性：正义
title_en: Learn about the importance of accessible product design at TechCrunch Sessions: Justice

note:
本周在Extra Crunch Live上，二人组解释了他们如何共同参与Grafana的A系列-以及最终的B系列。他们还带领我们走过了Grafana的原始A系列推销甲板，然后古普塔分享了对他而言突出的方面以及将这些观点传达给了Lightspeed更广泛的合作伙伴。
 古普塔（Gupta）和杜特（Dutt）还提供了听众提出的有关音高甲板的反馈，并分享了他们对什么造就了伟大的创始人演讲的看法，从而拉开了风险投资人实际上如何消费音高甲板的帷幕。
 我们在下面提供了重点内容以及对话的完整视频。
 我们每个星期三中午12点录制新的Extra Crunch Live情节。太平洋标准时间/下午3点美国东部标准时间晚上8点格林威治标准时间。在此处查看2月的时间表。
    古普塔（Gupta）在2019年6月加入Lightspeed时，他就开始追求杜特（Dutt）和格拉法娜（Grafana）实验室。他发短信，打电话和发电子邮件，但几乎没有回音。最终，他制定了与斯德哥尔摩的团队见面的计划，但即使如此，杜特还是反应迟钝。
 两人面带微笑地讲述了这个故事。杜特（Dutt）说，不仅他变得混乱不堪，而且对自己在斯德哥尔摩的联合创始人的出行计划也不完全确定，格拉法娜（Grafana）甚至都没有养育自己。尽管如此，古普塔坚持不懈，最终发送了一封严厉的电子邮件。
 “有一次，我就像‘Raj，算了吧。 “这行不通”，”古普塔回忆说。 “突然他醒了。”古普塔补充说，他生气了，“顺便说一句，这通常对风险投资人不起作用，但在这种情况下，这行之有效。” 
当他们终于见面时，他们相处了。 Dutt表示，由于Gupta在Splunk和Elastic这类组织内部的经验，他们能够进行购物。 古普塔将这次旅行描述为旋风，时间飞逝。  “我喜欢Gaurav的原因之一是他是一位新的VC，” Dutt解释道。 “所以对我来说，他似乎是我见过的最非VC VC之一。 这实际上很有吸引力。”  直到今天，古普塔和杜特还没有每周例行会议。 取而代之的是，他们每周演讲几次，就行业新闻，Grafana的产品以及公司的整体发展趋势进行有机对话。  Dutt与Extra Crunch Live观众分享了Grafana的Pre-Series A音高甲板，他实际上在他们见面之前就将其发送给了Gupta和Lightspeed。 但是，正如我们现在所知道的，正是杜特和古普塔（最终）进行的对话为这笔交易提供了火花。
note_en:
This week on  Extra Crunch Live, the duo explained how they came together for Grafana’s Series A — and eventually, its Series B. They also walked us through Grafana’s original Series A pitch deck before Gupta shared the aspects that stood out to him and how he communicated those points to the broader partnership at Lightspeed.
 Gupta and Dutt also offered feedback on pitch decks submitted by audience members and shared their thoughts about what makes a great founder presentation, pulling back the curtain on how VCs actually consume pitch decks.
 We’ve included highlights below as well as the full video of our conversation.
 We record new episodes of Extra Crunch Live each Wednesday at 12 p.m. PST/3 p.m. EST/8 p.m. GMT.  Check out the February schedule here.
    As soon as Gupta joined Lightspeed in June 2019, he began pursuing Dutt and Grafana Labs. He texted, called and emailed, but he got little to no response. Eventually, he made plans to go meet the team in Stockholm but, even then, Dutt wasn’t super responsive.
 The pair told the story with smiles on their faces. Dutt said that not only was he disorganized and not entirely sure of his own travel plans to see his co-founder in Stockholm, Grafana wasn’t even raising. Still, Gupta persisted and eventually sent a stern email.
 “At one point, I was like ‘Raj, forget it. This isn’t working’,” recalled Gupta. “And suddenly he woke up.” Gupta added that he got mad, which “usually does not work for VCs, by the way, but in this case, it kind of worked.”
 When they finally met, they got along. Dutt said they were able to talk shop due to Gupta’s experience inside organizations like Splunk and Elastic. Gupta described the trip as a whirlwind, where time just flew by.
 “One of the reasons that I liked Gaurav is that he was a new VC,” explained Dutt. “So to me, he seemed like one of the most non-VC VCs I’d ever met. And that was actually quite attractive.”
 To this day, Gupta and Dutt don’t have weekly standing meetings. Instead, they speak several times a week, conversing organically about industry news, Grafana’s products and the company’s overall trajectory.
  Dutt shared Grafana’s pre-Series A pitch deck — which he actually sent to Gupta and Lightspeed before they met — with the Extra Crunch Live audience. But as we know now, it was the conversations that Dutt and Gupta had (eventually) that provided the spark for that deal.
title: Lightspeed的Gaurav Gupta和Grafana的Raj Dutt讨论了音高甲板，定价以及如何完善叙述
title_en: Lightspeed’s Gaurav Gupta and Grafana’s Raj Dutt discuss pitch decks, pricing and how to nail the narrative
